moduleTitle: Evaluation of Base-Material Product Forms I: Ingots, Plate, Bar, and Pipe

lessons.title: Ingots: Discontinuities and Ultrasonic Response
objectives: Summarize the ingot casting process and where discontinuities originate
Predict the typical orientation of pipe, porosity, and nonmetallic inclusions in ingots
Evaluate ingot indications against applicable codes and standards
cp105Topics: 2.1.2 Types, origin, and typical orientation of discontinuities

lessons.title: Plate and Sheet: Rolling Process and Laminar Discontinuities
objectives: Explain how rolling elongates ingot discontinuities into laminations and stringers
Select straight-beam techniques for laminar discontinuity detection in plate
Apply common plate scanning standards and grid acceptance criteria
cp105Topics: 2.2.2 Types, origin, and typical orientation of discontinuities

lessons.title: Bar and Rod: Forming Process and Centerline Discontinuities
objectives: Describe how bar and rod forming orients discontinuities along the product axis
Recognize ultrasonic responses from centerline pipe, bursts, and stringers
Evaluate bar and rod indications against applicable codes and standards
cp105Topics: 2.3.2 Types, origin, and typical orientation of discontinuities

lessons.title: Pipe and Tubular Products: Manufacturing and Evaluation
objectives: Compare seamless and welded pipe manufacturing and their characteristic discontinuities
Set up circumferential and axial angle-beam scans using notch standards
Evaluate tubular product indications against applicable codes and standards
cp105Topics: 2.4.2 Types, origin, and typical orientation of discontinuities
